IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CIVIL APPEAL NO.        /2026
(arising out of SLP(C) NO. 17910/2026)
PREMAL PRATAP JOISHER & ANR. APPELLANT(S)
                VERSUS
SHRI JYOTHIRAADHITYA INFRA & ORS. RESPONDENT(S)
O R D E R
1. Leave granted. 
2. As the contesting parties are before this Court and are
agreeable, this matter is being disposed of today.
3. The challenge in this appeal is to the order dated 07.04.2026
passed by a Division Bench of the High Court of Andhra Pradesh
in Writ Appeal No. 285/2026. 
4. The said appeal was filed against the order dated 22.04.2025
passed by a learned Judge in Writ Petition No. 8881/2025. By
the  said  order,  the  learned  Judge  took  note  of  the
unconditional apology tendered by the Tahsildar, Somandepalli
Village and Mandal, Sri Satya Sai District, in relation to the
impugned notice issued by him on 15.03.2025, despite the
matter being  sub judice . The learned Judge also noted the
acknowledgement by the Tahsildar and his counsel that such
action on his part was without jurisdiction. The learned
1

--- Page 436 ---
Judge, accordingly, quashed the said notice and allowed the
writ petition. 
5. Surprisingly, by the impugned order dated 07.04.2026, the
Division  Bench  restored  the  said  notice  and  directed
appropriate action to be taken thereon. 
6. We may note that this Court had occasion to deal with an
offshoot  of  this  litigation  in  Civil  Appeal  Nos.  4577-
4578/2026,  which  were  disposed  of  by  judgment  dated
17.04.2026. This Court made it clear therein that, even if the
appellate authority under the A ndhra Pradesh Rights in Land
and Pattadar Pass Books Act, 1971, deals with the appeal
pending before him on its merits and in accordance with law,
the said authority should be mindful of the fact that the
substantial  appeal  in  A.S.  No.  101/2022  was  pending
consideration before the High Court and that any decision
rendered by the said authority would be subject to and would
have to abide by the final decision in the said first appeal.
It was, however, left open to the appellate authority to pass
appropriate orders with regard to the entries to be made in
the Revenue records, after hearing all the parties concerned,
on merits and in accordance with law. 
7. This being the situation, it was not open to the Tahsildar,
Somandepalli Village and Mandal, Sri Satya Sai District, to
initiate a fresh inquiry into the documents sought to be
relied  upon  by  the  appellants  under  threat  of  criminal
prosecution. The learned judge was, therefore, fully justified
2

--- Page 437 ---
in quashing the notice issued by the said Tahsildar, upon
accepting his apology and his acknowledgment that he had no
jurisdiction to issue such a notice. 
8. The Division Bench, therefore, erred in reversing the said
order and restoring the notice for further action to be taken
thereon. 
9. The appeal is accordingly allowed, setting aside the impugned
order dated 07.04.2026 passed by the Division Bench of the
High Court of Andhra Pradesh in Writ Appeal No. 285/2026. 
10. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of. 
..........................J.
(SANJAY KUMAR)
..........................J.
(K. VINOD CHANDRAN)
NEW DELHI;
MAY 25, 2026.
3

--- Page 438 ---
ITEM NO.25               COURT NO.12               SECTION XII-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No. 17910/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 07-04-2026 in WA
No. 285/2026 passed by the High Court of Andhra Pradesh at Amravati]
PREMAL PRATAP JOISHER & ANR.                       Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
SHRI JYOTHIRAADHITYA INFRA & ORS.                  Respondent(s)
(IA No. 154856/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED JUDGMENT
and IA No. 154857/2026 - PERMISSION TO FILE LENGTHY LIST OF DATES)
 
Date : 25-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CRIMINAL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CRIMINAL APPEAL NO.       /2026
  [@ SLP [CRL.] NO.4087/2026]
MAMTA RANI                      Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF RAJASTHAN & ANR.        Respondent(s)
O R D E R
Leave granted.
The  appellant  seeks  regular  bail  in
connection with FIR No.739/2023 dated 16.11.2023,
registered at Police Station Hanumangarh Junction,
District – Hanumangarh, for the offence punishable
under Section 302, 364, 120-B and 34 of the Indian
Penal Code, 1860.
We have heard the learned counsel appearing
for the parties.
Though it is submitted by the learned counsel
appearing for the State that the trial has already
commenced and the witnesses are being examined,

--- Page 452 ---
2
the fact remains that the appellant is a lady
having minor children. She has already undergone
incarceration for a period of two and a half
years, approximately.
Considering  the  above  position,  we  are
inclined to grant bail to the appellant. 
Accordingly, the impugned order stands set
aside and the appellant is granted bail on the
terms and conditions subject to the satisfaction
of the concerned Trial Court.
The appeal stands allowed, accordingly.
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand
disposed of.
.......................J.
[M.M. SUNDRESH]
.......................J.
[NONGMEIKAPAM KOTISWAR SINGH]
NEW DELHI;
MAY 26, 2026.

PUNEET TYAGI @ PUNIT TYAGI      Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF HARYANA                  Respondent(s)
O R D E R
Leave granted.
The appellant seeks bail in connection with
FIR No.2392/2019, dated 13.12.2019, registered at
Police Station Shivaji Nagar, District- Gurugram,
for the offences punishable under Sections 306,
419, 420, 467, 468, 471, 120B and 201 of the
Indian Penal Code, 1860.  
We have heard the learned counsel appearing
for the parties.
The case against the appellant is that the
suicide note written by the deceased mentions the
appellant’s phone number, and that the appellant

--- Page 456 ---
2
was  a  tele-caller  who  called  the  deceased
demanding money.
Considering  the  facts  governing  and  the
period  of  incarceration  undergone  by  the
appellant, coupled with the fact that the co-
accused have been granted bail, we are inclined to
grant bail to the appellant.
 Accordingly, the impugned order is set aside,
and the appellant is granted bail on terms and
conditions to the satisfaction of the concerned
Trial Court.
The appeal stands allowed, accordingly.
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand
disposed of.

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CRIMINAL ORIGINAL JURISDICTION
TRANSFER PETITION(S)(CRIMINAL)  NO(S).  92/2026
S.V. SRINIVASA RAO                       PETITIONER(S)
                              VERSUS
CENTRAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION, 
NEW DELHI            RESPONDENT(S)
O R D E R
1. Heard  Ms. Amrita Sharma, learned counsel appearing for
the  petitioner  and  Mr.  Anil  Kaushik,  learned  Additional
Solicitor  General  appearing  for  the  Central  Bureau  of
Investigation (for short, ‘CBI’).
2. By this Transfer Petition, the petitioner seeks transfer
of CBI Case bearing No.CBI/20/2020 titled as "Central Bureau Of
Investigation Vs. S.V. Srinivasa Rao”, pending before the Court
of Chief Judicial Magistrate, Rouse Avenue Court at New Delhi
to the Chief Judicial Magistrate at Bengaluru, Karnataka.
3. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the
petitioner is a octogenarian and has been suffering from
several ailments. Learned counsel for the petitioner seeks

--- Page 464 ---
2
transfer on the said ground.
4. We enquired from the learned Additional Solicitor General
as to how many witnesses are to be examined and as to where
they are located.
5. Learned  Additional  Solicitor  General  with  his  usual
fairness  submitted  that  08  out  of  the  11  witnesses  are
residents of Bengaluru, Karnataka. From this 08 witnesses, 07
are official witnesses and 01 is a public witness.
6. Considering that large number of witnesses are based in
Bengaluru, Karnataka and the agency(CBI) also has a detachment
in Bengaluru, Karnataka and will not be put to any difficulty,
we are of the opinion that interest of justice would be sub-
served, if the Transfer Petition is allowed.
7. Accordingly,  we  direct  that  the  CBI  Case  bearing
No.CBI/20/2020  titled as "Central Bureau Of Investigation Vs.
S.V. Srinivasa Rao”, pending before the Court of Chief Judicial
Magistrate, Rouse Avenue Court at New Delhi, shall stand
transferred to the Principal District Judge, Bengaluru City,
Karnataka,  who  shall  assign  it  to  a  Court  of  competent
jurisdiction.
8. The transferor Court shall forthwith transmit the record
of the aforesaid case to the transferee court.
9. In view the nature of the matter, we also direct that the
transferee Court may endeavour to decide the case within a
period of one year from today.
10. With the above observations, the Transfer Petition is
disposed of.

--- Page 465 ---
3
11. Pending application(s), if any, shall also stand disposed
of. 
………………………………………………………….J.
[K.V. VISWANATHAN]
 ……………………………………………………...J.
    [VIJAY BISHNOI]
NEW DELHI;
MAY 22, 2026.

--- Page 466 ---
4

Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.)

[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order 
dated  17-11-2025 in MCRC No. 46737/2025 passed by
the High Court of Madhya Pradesh Principal Seat at
Jabalpur]
NARAYAN SINGH SONDHIYA               Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH          Respondent(s)
 
Date : 27-05-2026 This petition was called on for 
hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.K. MAHESHWARI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s) : Mrs. Anchal Maheshwari, Adv.
Mr. Keshav Baheti, Adv.
Mr. Dlwan Singh, Adv.
Mr. Ashutosh Thakur, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : Mr. Manisha T Karia, A.A.G.
                   Ms. Mrinal Gopal Elker, AOR
                   Mr. Rajan K Chaurasia, Adv.
                   Mr. Sai Shashank, Adv.
                   Ms. Silpi S Swain, Adv.
                   Ms. Archana, Adv.
                   
UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the
following
O R D E R
1SLP (Crl.)  No.6447/2026

--- Page 472 ---
1. Against an order rejecting the regular
bail by the High Court and to seek bail,
the present special leave petition has been
filed.  The petitioner is in custody w.e.f.
13.05.2023 in connection with FIR No.458 of
2022  dated  14.10.2022  for  the  offences
punishable under Sections 368, 370, 373,
376(3), 376(2)(N) and 354K of the Indian
Penal Code, 1860 and Section 5L and 6 of
the  Protection  of  Children  from  Sexual
Offences Act and Section 3(2)(v) of the
Scheduled  Castes  and  Scheduled  Tribes
(Prevention of atrocities) Act and Section
9 of the Bal Vivah Parishodh Adhiniyam
registered  with  Police  Station
vijayraghavagarh,  District  Katni,  Madhya
Pradesh.
2SLP (Crl.)  No.6447/2026

--- Page 473 ---
2. After hearing learned counsel for the
parties and considering the sequence of
events and the role as assigned to the
petitioner in shifting the victim and the
period of custody which is more than three
years.  Charges were framed on 05.08.2023
and now even after a lapse of two and a
half years, only 13 out of 26 witnesses
have been examined.  In such circumstances,
it cannot be ruled out that the trial may
take some time to conclude.  Hence, without
expressing any opinion on the merits, we
deem  it  appropriate  to  release  the
petitioner on bail.
3. Accordingly,  we  direct  that  the
petitioner  be  released  on  bail  on
furnishing  the  suitable  bail  bonds  and
sureties  and  on  such  other  terms  and
3SLP (Crl.)  No.6447/2026

--- Page 474 ---
conditions as may be deemed fit by the
trial Court.
4. Petitioner to abide all the conditions
as imposed and shall regularly attend the
trial  until  exempted  by  the  Court.
Violation, if any, may give a cause to take
recourse as permissible and the trial Court
is at liberty to do the needful.
5. Accordingly  and  in  view  of  the
foregoing,  the  special  leave  petition
stands allowed.
6. Pending application(s), if any, shall
stand disposed of.
(GULSHAN KUMAR ARORA)          (NAND KISHOR)
  DEPUTY REGISTRAR          ASSISTANT REGISTRAR
4SLP (Crl.)  No.6447/2026

1SLP (Crl.)  No(s).  5765/2026

--- Page 476 ---
the present special leave petition has been
filed.  The petitioner is in custody w.e.f.
08.06.2023 in connection with FIR No.200 of
2023  dated  07.06.2023  for  the  offences
punishable under Sections 498-A and 304-B
of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 and Section
3/4 of the Dowry Prohibition Act registered
with Police Station Gautam Buddha Nagar,
district  Central  Commissionerate,  Uttar
Pradesh.
2. In the instance case, the petitioner
is  the  husband  and  cause  of  death  is
Asphyxia.  The period of custody is more
than two years and 11 months, charges were
framed on 9.11.2023 and by this time only
seven  out  of  43  witnesses  have  been

2SLP (Crl.)  No(s).  5765/2026

--- Page 477 ---
directions issued by the High Court on

is not informed that after such direction,
how many witnesses have been examined by
the prosecution.  Considering the aforesaid
and the period of custody and also the fact
that from the date of framing of the charge
despite lapse of two and a half years only
seven  witnesses  have  been  examined.
Therefore, delay in trial cannot be ruled
out.   Considering  all  the  attending
circumstances, we deem it appropriate to
release the petitioner on bail.

petitioner  be  released  on  bail  on

1SLP (Crl) No.5532/2026

--- Page 480 ---
1. Against an order rejecting the regular
bail by the High Court and to seek bail,
the present special leave petition has been
filed.  The petitioner is in custody w.e.f.
12.08.2025 in connection with FIR No 248
dated  01.12.2021  for  the  offences
punishable under Sections 408, 420 and 120-
B of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 registered
with  Police  Station  Sahnewal,  District
Ludhiana, Punjab.
2. In the instant case, the petitioner
was initially granted anticipatory bail but
because of declaration of the proclaimed
offender on 17.05.2025, he was arrested on
12.08.2025.  Since that time, he is in
custody.  The investigation is complete.
Charge-sheet is filed and the charges have
already been framed and out of six, one
2SLP (Crl) No.5532/2026

--- Page 481 ---
witness has been examined.  The entire case
is based on documentary evidence and for
non-cooperation,  he  has  been  put  into
custody for nine months after granting the
anticipatory bail as per the orders of the
court.  Considering the said mitigating
circumstances,  at  present,  without
expressing any opinion on the merits of the
case, we deem it appropriate to release the
petitioner on bail.
3. Accordingly,  we  direct  that  the
petitioner  be  released  on  bail  on
furnishing  the  suitable  bail  bonds  and
sureties  and  on  such  other  terms  and

trial Court.

as imposed and shall regularly attend and

needful.

foregoing,  the  special  leave  petition
stands allowed.

stand disposed of.

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CRIMINAL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CRIMINAL APPEAL NO.            /2026
[@ SPECIAL LEAVE PETITION (CRIMINAL) NO.6194/2026]
VIKAS SINGH CHANDEL                 Appellant(s)
                      VERSUS
STATE OF MADHYA PRADESH             Respondent(s)
O R D E R
1. Leave granted.
2. Heard learned counsel for the parties.
3. This  appeal  arises  from  an  order  dated
20.01.2026 passed by the High Court of Madhya
Pradesh rejecting the regular bail prayer of the
appellant  in  connection  with  FIR  No.589/2024
registered at Police Station Majhauli, District
Sidhi.
4. At the outset, the learned counsel for the
appellant submits that the appellant has suffered
incarceration  for  about  two  years  and  the
allegation against the appellant is of carrying 40
bottles of Onrex cough syrup containing Codeine
Phosphate.  It has been submitted that the trial
is nowhere near conclusion and, therefore, the
appellant may be released on bail having regard to
Crl.A. @ SLP (Crl.) No.6194/2026                                                     1

--- Page 488 ---
the nature of contraband seized.
5. The learned counsel for the State has opposed
the prayer for bail.
6. Having regard to the nature of the contraband
seized as also that the appellant by now has
suffered about two years of incarceration pending
trial, we are of the view that the appellant is
entitled to be released on bail.
7. The  appeal  is,  accordingly,  allowed.  The
order passed by the High Court rejecting the bail
prayer of the appellant is set aside.
8. The appellant shall be released on bail on
such terms and conditions as the trial court may
deem fit to impose in the facts and circumstances
of the case.
9. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand
disposed of.
……………………………………………………J
                   [MANOJ MISRA]
……………………………………………………J
                          [MANMOHAN]
New Delhi
MAY 26, 2026
Crl.A. @ SLP (Crl.) No.6194/2026                                                     2

--- Page 489 ---

                     VERSUS

O R D E R
1. By this petition, petitioner-wife seeks transfer
of Matrimonial Case No. 911 of 2025, titled as “ Amit
Baghel  Vs  Vineeta ”  pending  before  the  Court  of
Principal Judge, Family Court, Aligarh, Uttar Pradesh
to the Court of Family Judge, Karkardooma Court, New
Delhi.
2. Though service is complete, none appears for
respondent.
3. Upon consideration of the submissions and perusal
of materials available on record, we are of the view
that this Transfer Petition deserves to be allowed.
Accordingly, Matrimonial Case No. 911 of 2025, titled
as “Amit Baghel Vs Vineeta” pending before the Court of
Principal Judge, Family Court, Aligarh, Uttar Pradesh
is hereby transferred to the Court of Family Judge,
Karkardooma  Court,  New  Delhi.  The  transferor  Court
1

--- Page 492 ---
shall transmit the entire record of the case forthwith
to the transferee Court.
4. It will be open to the parties to participate in
the  proceedings  through  video  conferencing  platform
except on occasions where their physical presence is
necessary.
5. The Transfer Petition is allowed in the above
terms.
6. Pending  application(s),  if  any  shall  stand
disposed of.

ITEM NO.9               COURT NO.1               SECTION PIL-W
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Writ Petition(s)(Civil)  No(s).  322/2026
CHARANJEET SINGH & ANR.                            Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
UNION OF INDIA & ORS.                              Respondent(s)
(IA No. 81111/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 20-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIPUL M. PANCHOLI
For Petitioner(s) :  Petitioner-in-person
                    
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The instant Writ Petition under Article 32 of the Constitution
of India, purportedly filed in public interest, seeks wide-ranging
directions  concerning  the  identification,  preservation,  audit,
management,  investigation,  restitution  and  protection  of  Sikh
religious, historical and endowment properties, including what has
been described as “Khalsayi Sikh heritage properties”. The petition
also contains broader prayers concerning religious properties,
trusts, boards, committees, NGOs and allied institutions across the
country.
2. We have heard the petitioner appearing in person and perused
the material placed on record.
1

--- Page 504 ---
3. The concerns voiced by the petitioner relate, broadly, to
preservation of religious and heritage properties, transparency in
their management, prevention of encroachment and alienation, and
accountability of persons or bodies entrusted with such properties.
These are undoubtedly matters of public significance. However, the
reliefs as framed are omnibus in nature and traverse several
distinct fields, including the realms of legislative policy and
executive administration.
4. There is no gainsaying that such reliefs cannot be granted by
this Court in the abstract, in the absence of a defined statutory
duty, specific factual pleadings, identifiable property-wise cause
of action, necessary parties, and prior recourse to the competent
authorities. 
5. We also find that, before invoking the writ jurisdiction of
this  Court,  the  petitioner  has  not  placed  on  record  any
comprehensive representation made to the competent authorities
seeking consideration of the issues now raised in this petition.
The petition itself proceeds on broad assertions and illustrative
grievances, but does not lay the foundation necessary for issuance
of writs of the nature sought.
6. We have, therefore, impressed upon the petitioner that the
appropriate course, at the first instance, would be to place a
comprehensive and properly documented representation before the
2

--- Page 505 ---
competent authorities. It shall also be open to the petitioner, if
so advised, to submit a petition before the Committee on Petitions
of the Lok Sabha, which is constituted under Rule 306 of the Rules
of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, and whose scope
and functions are traceable to Rule 307 read with Direction 95 of
the Directions by the Speaker, Lok Sabha.
7. Insofar  as  any  specific  property,  institution,  trust,
gurdwara, endowment or statutory body is concerned, it will be open
to the petitioner to avail of the remedies available in law before
the competent forum, including the jurisdictional High Court, civil
court, revenue authority, statutory authority, police/investigating
agency, or any other forum competent to examine such grievance on
facts.
8. We make it clear that we have expressed no opinion on the
merits of the allegations made in the petition, including with
respect to any particular property, institution, committee, board,
trust, public authority or private person. All questions of fact
and law are left open.
9. If, after a properly supported representation is made, any
competent authority fails to consider the same in accordance with
law within a reasonable time, or passes an order giving rise to a
fresh and concrete cause of action, it shall be open to the
petitioner to pursue his remedy as may be available in law, before
the  jurisdictional  High  Court(s).  This  liberty  shall  not  be
3

--- Page 506 ---
construed as liberty to revive the present petition in its present
omnibus form.
10. The instant petition is, accordingly, disposed of with the
aforesaid liberty.
11. Pending interlocutory applications, if any, stand disposed of.
(NITIN TALREJA)                              (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        ASSISTANT REGISTRAR
4

--- Page 507 ---

20.05.2026 in CRM-M No.23681/2026 passed by the High Court
of Punjab & Haryana at Chandigarh.

3. Issue notice, returnable on 17.07.2026.

1

--- Page 508 ---
service be taken within one (01) week.
5. Mr. Samar Vijay Singh, learned counsel, accepts notice
on behalf of respondent no.1/State. Hence, the formal
service of notice on the said respondent is waived.
6. Learned counsel for the petitioner to serve a copy of
the present petition to learned counsel for respondent no.1
forthwith.
7. Respondent No.1 shall file counter affidavit within
three (03) weeks. Rejoinder affidavit thereto, if any, be
filed before the next date of listing.
8. We make it clear that no further opportunity will be
granted for this purpose.
9. In  the  notice  itself,  let  it  be  mentioned  that
respondent no.2 is required to file the counter affidavit
and reply to the interlocutory application(s), if any,
before the next date of listing.
10. In the event of arrest in connection with Crime No.90
of 2026 of Sonepat City Police Station, Sonepat District,
Haryana, the petitioner shall be released on bail on the
appropriate terms and conditions as may be fixed by the
Investigating Officer/Arresting Officer.  However, in the
attending facts, one of the conditions shall necessarily be
that if the petitioner possesses a passport, the same shall
be deposited before the Competent Authority/Court, till the
conclusion of the Trial or directed otherwise.
11. The petitioner shall make himself available before the
Investigating Officer on 05.06.2026 at 10:00 a.m. and on
2

--- Page 509 ---
all such dates as he may be required.
12. Needless to add, till the investigation is complete in
all respects, the petitioner would fully cooperate, and if
the challan/charge sheet is filed, he would maintain good
conduct and not attempt to influence any of the witnesses
in any manner till the completion of the trial.

1. Hon’ble  Mr.  Justice  J.B.  Pardiwala  pronounced  the
reportable  judgment of the Bench comprising His Lordship and
Hon’ble Mr. Justice K.V. Viswanathan.
2. Leave granted.
3. The relevant part of the signed reportable judgment is as
under:-
“In view of what has been pointed out hereinabove,
we are of the opinion that, for a comprehensive
consideration and an authoritative pronouncement
after taking into account all aspects, including
those dealt with hereinabove, the matter needs to
be placed before Hon’ble the Chief Justice of
India to constitute an appropriate three-Judge
Bench.  
2

--- Page 515 ---
SLP(Crl.)  Nos.12135-12136/2024
The three-Judge Bench constituted may refer to the
following questions:
(i) Whether the provisions of Section 138 of
the  NI  Act  and  the  objective  underlying  the
enactment  thereof  indicate  that  it  is  quasi-
criminal  in  nature  with  a  tilt  towards  the
criminal side?
(ii) Whether  the  moratorium  provisions  under
Part III of the IBC should be made applicable on
the entire proceedings under Section 138 of the NI
Act or only to the compensatory aspect thereof?
The registry is directed to place these matters before
Hon'ble  the  Chief  Justice of  India  for  appropriate
orders.”
 
(CHANDRESH)                                     (POOJA SHARMA)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

--- Page 517 ---
                   Mr. Manish Kumar, Adv.
                   
                 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner has been denied regular bail by the High
Court in connection with Case Crime No. 599/2021, registered
with Sikahdarabad Police Station, District Bulandshahr, State of
U.P., for the offence punishable under Sections 323 and 307 of
the Indian Penal Code, 1860, respectively (for short, “the
IPC”).
2. The completion of investigation and filing of charge-sheet
culminated in Sessions Case No.2081/2022 pending as on date in
the Court of 2 nd Additional District and Sessions Judge Court
No.1, Bulandshahr, State of Uttar Pradesh.
3. We take into consideration the following aspects:-
(I) The petitioner is in judicial custody as an under
trial prisoner since almost 05 years.
(II) Till  this date  the prosecution  has been  able to
examine 05 witnesses.
4. The learned counsel appearing for the State would submit
SLP(Crl.)  No.6896/2026 2

--- Page 518 ---
that the prosecution intends to examine in all 22 witnesses.
5. This  being  a  case  one  of  attempt  to  commit  murder
punishable under Section 307 of the IPC, we are persuaded to
exercise our discretion in favour of the petitioner, more
particularly, when a similarly situated co-accused, namely,
Rambhool, is on bail. 
6. We order that the petitioner be released on bail forthwith,
if not required in any other case, subject to the terms and
conditions that the Trial Court may deem fit to impose. Despite
the fact that we have ordered release of the petitioner on bail,
trial shall proceed further expeditiously.
7. With the aforesaid, the Special Leave Petition stands
disposed of.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(CHANDRESH)                                     (POOJA SHARMA)

1

--- Page 520 ---
4. Learned counsel appearing for the petitioner submits

High Court  vide order dated 11.08.2017 in W.P.No.210 of
2016 (page No.110) had quashed the order of sanction dated

listing. In the counter affidavit, it shall be indicated
whether the respondents have accepted the order dated

--- Page 521 ---

allowed.

No. 153/2018, titled “State Vs. Mandeep & Ors.”, pending before
the Court of CJ(JD) cum JMIC, Jhajjar, Haryana  is transferred

1) After hearing learned counsel, we see no reason to
entertain the present special leave petition.  Accordingly,
the  special  leave  petition  is  dismissed.   Pending
application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
2) However, the dismissal of this special leave petition
would not debar the petitioner to apply before the High
Court seeking discharge or to take any defence as available
to her under law. 
SLP (Crl.) No. 10229/2026
3) The instant petition is arising out of an order of
issuance of non-bailable warrant of arrest, however, learned
counsel for the petitioner states that she shall appear
before Police Station Shivpur, District Varanasi, on 01 st
June, 2026, at 11 a.m.  We take the said statement on
record.  
4) On  her  appearance,  the  Investigating  Officer  may
proceed in the investigation and act in accordance with law.
5) In view of the said observation, the order impugned
shall be kept in abeyance.  In case the petitioner does not
appear on the date and time as specified hereinabove, the
order impugned shall be given effect to.
6) The  special  leave  petition  stands  disposed  of

ITEM NO.75               COURT NO.13               SECTION II
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
CONMT.PET.(C) No.173/2026 in SLP(Crl) No.2303/2024
RUBINA KHAN                                        Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
NADEEM TARIQ                    Alleged Contemnor(s)/Respondent(s)
(IA No. 72051/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 27-05-2026 This matter was called for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE R. MAHADEVAN
For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Yusuf, AoR
                   Mr. Amarjeet Singh Girsa, Adv.
                   Mrs. Ritu Solanki, Adv.
                   Mr. Amit Arora, Adv.
                   Mr. Jatin Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Amit Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Deepak Dahiya, Adv.
                   Mr. Rahul Pandey, Adv.
                   Mr. Shivee Pandey, Adv.
                   
                   
For Respondent(s)  Mr. Rizwan, Adv.
                   Ms. Vanya Gupta, AoR
                   Ms. Sachi Chopra, Adv.
                   Ms. Jessica Khera, Adv.                   
                   
          
                             O R D E R
Heard learned counsels for the parties.
2. In  terms  of  our  earlier  order  dated  13.04.2026,  the
respondent-alleged contemnor is also present in the Court. He has
also filed a show-cause. It is a sorry state of affairs when the

--- Page 547 ---
2
respondent, an enrolled Advocate commits contempt of the order of
the Court by not complying with it. Though, an affidavit has been
filed stating that now, the said order has been complied in full,
there is no clarity as the learned counsel for the petitioner
submits that still, full payment has not been made.
3. We are surprised, as to how, even when the respondent was
summoned by this Court in a Contempt Petition, he does not
understand the seriousness and still, has not come clean for the
reason that the petitioner has issues with regard to non-compliance
of the order of this Court. This indicates that the respondent is
trying to take advantage of his position and is not being fair to
the Court.
4. The Court at this juncture, may observe that it would be more
strict with a person, who is aware of his duty to comply with the
order of the Court especially, when he is an enrolled Advocate
himself. Thus, the Court called upon the learned counsel for the
respondent to address on why the respondent be not proceeded for
contempt. 
5. Accordingly,  notice  in  contempt  is  issued  against  the
respondent. The Court was further of the view that some interim
strict order be passed.
6. At this juncture, learned counsel for the respondent and the
respondent prayed for one lat indulgence.
7. Accordingly, by way of mercy, we direct the matter to be
listed on 29.05.2026, at the top of the Board.
8. Before the next date of listing, the entire dues as per the
order of the Trial Court be cleared and an affidavit filed showing

--- Page 548 ---
3
compliance enclosing the chart. If the same is not done, the Court
may proceed to pass strict order.
(SAPNA BISHT)                                   (ANJALI PANWAR)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CIVIL APPEAL NOs.        OF 2026
(Arising out of SLP (Civil) Nos. 38706-38707 of 2025)
SHUAIB & ORS.  APPELLANT(S)
VERSUS
SHAIMA RESPONDENT(S)
O R D E R
1. Leave granted.
2. The  appellants  challenge  the  judgment  dated
08.10.2025 in Mat.Appeal Nos. 304/2021 and 230/2021 passed
by the High Court of Judicature at Kerala at Ernakulam.
3. Operative part of the impugned order is reproduced
as under :-
“a) 1st respondent shall return 60 sovereigns
of gold ornaments or its market value as on
the date of return.
b) The judgment and decree of the Family
Court directing the 1 st respondent to return
Rs. 5,50,000/- with 6% interest from the date
of  filing  of  the  Original  Petition  till
realisation stands confirmed.
c) The other claims sought by the petitioner
stands dismissed.
d)  Petitioner  is  entitled  to  get
proportionate cost through out.”
 
4. We are happy to record the efforts put in by the
learned counsel for the parties in having the matter
amicably resolved.
5. Appellant No.1-former husband has agreed to pay a
sum of Rs. 50,00,000/- (Rupees Fifty Lakh Only) as a lump

--- Page 568 ---
sum to the respondent-former wife.
6. This is towards settlement of all claims, be it of
whatever nature, as set up by the respondent herein, who
has agreed to accept the same, also towards satisfaction of
the judgment and decree impugned herein.
7. Appellant No. 1 Mr. Shuaib undertakes to pay the
aforesaid amount to the respondent Ms. Shaima within a
period of three months from today. The amount shall be paid
in three equal installments.  The first installment shall
be paid within next seven days.
8. In this regard, an undertaking shall be furnished by
appellant No. 1 before the Registry of this court within
two weeks. 
9. The amount shall be directly remitted into the bank
account of the respondent-former wife. The bank details
shall be supplied by the learned counsel for the respondent
to the learned counsel for the appellants.
10. The impugned judgment and Decree dated 08.10.2025
stands modified accordingly.
11. The appeals are partly allowed in the above terms.
12. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of.

             [SANJAY KAROL]
   
    ………………………………………………………J.

NEW DELHI;
13 May, 2026

Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No.  18518/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 18-03-2026
in FAFO No. 184/2025 passed by the High Court of Judicature at
Allahabad, Lucknow Bench]
ICICI LOMBARD GENERAL INSURANCE CO. LTD.           Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
SHASHI BALA & ORS.                                 Respondent(s)
(IA No. 160525/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT; IA No. 160524/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.)
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PANKAJ MITHAL
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE S.V.N. BHATTI
For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Shoeb Alam, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Ishaan Chhaya, Adv.
                   Ms. Tanya Chaudhry, AOR                   
                   
For Respondent(s)  Mr. Santosh Kumar Pandey, AOR                 
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Heard Mr. Shoeb Alam, learned senior counsel
appearing for the petitioner.
2. Learned  counsel  for  the  petitioner  is
permitted to implead the State of Uttar Pradesh as a
party-respondent as the State had filed an affidavit
before the High Court.
3. The amended cause title be filed within a
week.

--- Page 571 ---
2
4. The submission of Mr. Shoeb Alam, learned
senior counsel for the petitioner is that it is one
of those cases where the fraud has been played upon
and the same vehicle has been involved in three
different accidents.
5. From the affidavit of the Superintendent of
Police, Local Police Department, Government of Uttar
Pradesh, it is evident that the said vehicle was
involved in three Accident Claim Petitions being
Claim  Petition  No.  408/2018  (Faizabad),  Claim
Petition No. 134/2018 (Faizabad) and Claim Petition
No. 240/2021 (Barabanki). In respect of all these
claim petitions, separate FIRs were lodged with delay
and there appears to be no eye-witnesses. In all the
three cases, Police had submitted charge sheet.
6. We would like to know from the State as to
what action has been taken by it against the officers
who submitted the charge sheets in the above three
cases when in the opinion of the SSP, the above
exercise is fraudulent in nature.
7. Issue notice.
8. Learned counsel appearing for the claimant may
also seek instructions and file counter affidavit so
also the State within a period of four weeks from
today.
9. List immediately on the expiry of the above

--- Page 572 ---
3
period.
10. On the next occasion, after the reply of the
State is filed, the Court will consider tagging of
this petition with SLP(C) No. 2795/2026 whereunder
such fraudulent cases are being separately monitored.
11. In the meantime, there shall be stay of the
impugned  order  of  the  Tribunal  dated  09.01.2025
provided  Insurance  Company  deposits  half  of  the
amount awarded with 7 per cent interest per annum on
the said half amount within a period of one month
from today before the Trial Court.
12. On deposit of the said amount, it shall be
invested in a fixed deposit with a nationalized bank
and shall not be released in favour of the claimants
without the leave of the Court.
(SNEHA DAS)                                (NIDHI MATHUR)
SENIOR PERSONAL ASSISTANT                     COURT MASTER (NSH)

UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the
following
                             
O R D E R
Leave granted.
The appellant was convicted by
The trial Court for the offences
punishable under Sections 302, 449
and 506  of the Indian Penal Code,
1860 and Section 27 of the Arms
Act, 1959 and sentenced to life
imprisonment.
The appeal preferred by the
appellant before the High Court has
been dismissed vide the impugned
order. Aggrieved by the same, the
appellant is before us. 
SLP (Crl.) No.3823/2026 2/3

--- Page 578 ---
The  appellant  has  already
undergone more than 17 years of
incarceration.
Considering the above, we are
inclined to suspend the sentence of
the appellant and grant bail to
him, during the pendency of this
appeal.
Accordingly,  the  sentence
imposed upon the appellant stands
suspended during the pendency of
this appeal and he is granted bail,
subject to the terms and conditions
that may be imposed by the Trial
Court.

O R D E R
IA No. 128934/2026 in Crl. A. No. 680/2024 & IA

The  applicants  in  the  above  listed
applciations  have  been  convicted  by  the  trial
Court for the offences punishable under  Sections
148, 149, 302 and 307  of the Indian Pe nal Code,
1860, and have been sentenced to undergo life
imprisonment. 
The  appeals  preferred  by  the  applicants
challenging the conviction, before the High Court,
came to be dismissed. Aggrieved by the same, they
are before us. 
It is not in dispute that the sentence,
insofar  as  the  co-accused  is  concerned,  is
suspended. We are also conscious of the fact that
the occurrence was of the year 1996, and the
applicants  are  already  under  incarceration  for
quite sometime.
Considering the above, we are inclined to
suspend the sentence of the applicants, and grant
bail to them, during the pendency of these appeals.
2

--- Page 581 ---
Accordingly, the sentence imposed upon the
applicants stand suspended, and they are granted
bail, subject to the terms and conditions that may
be imposed by the trial Court, during the pendency
of the appeal.

                   Mr. Adarsh Dubey, Adv.
UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the
following
O R D E R
1. Leave granted.
2. Heard on I.A. No. 59274 of 2026 for grant of
bail.
3. After  hearing  learned  counsel  for  the
parties, we see that the High Court converted the
conviction under Section 304 Part I and directed
to undergo the sentence of ten years and the
punishment for other offences, out of which the
appellant has served the actual sentence of five
years  and  eight  months  by  now,  along  with
remission it may be more than six and a half
years. Considering the aforesaid and the medical
condition of the appellant, during the pendency of
this appeal, without expressing any opinion on the
merits, we deem it appropriate to release the
appellant on bail.
2SLP (Crl) No.8166/2016

--- Page 584 ---
3.  Accordingly, we direct that the appellant be
released on bail on such terms and conditions as
may be deemed fit and imposed by the trial court.
4. We clarify and direct that after the release,
in case the appellant is found indulged in any
criminal case, the State Government would be at
liberty to take recourse as permissible seeking
cancellation of suspension of sentence before the
High Court.
5. Accordingly,  the  I.A.  for  bail  stands
disposed of.
6. List the appeal in due course.
(GULSHAN KUMAR ARORA)            (NAND KISHOR)

IA No. 131814/2026 - APPLICATION FOR SETTLEMENT
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PRASHANT KUMAR MISHRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE N.V. ANJARIA
For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Amar Dave, Sr. Adv.

For Respondent(s)  Mr. C. Paramasivam, Adv.
                   Mr. Rakesh K. Sharma, AOR
                   
                   Mr. S. S. Shroff, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Mr. Amar Dave, learned senior counsel represents the
petitioner and Mr. C. Paramasivam, learned senior counsel
represents Life Insurance Corporation of India. No one
appears for the intervenor Future Retail Limited. It is
informed that Future Retail Limited has already handed over
the possession of the property to the petitioner in the
1

--- Page 589 ---
month of July, 2022.
2. This Special Leave Petition is directed against the
order passed by the High Court whereby the petitioner’s
application under Article 227 of the Constitution of India
has been dismissed. The said petition before the High Court
in turn had challenged the order passed by the Appellate
Authority under Order 9 of the Public Premises (Eviction of
Unauthorized Occupants) Act, 1971, affirming the order of
eviction passed by the Estate Officer.
3. During pendency of the Special Leave Petition, the
parties have amicably settled the matter and a deed of
settlement has been executed between them which is annexed
in IA No. 131814 of 2026, a joint application on behalf of
the parties for disposal of the Special Leave Petition
pursuant to the mutually agreed settled terms between the
parties.
4. In  terms  of  the  settlement  the  Life  Insurance
Corporation of India (LIC)  has already executed a fresh
lease deed in favour of the petitioner and the  LIC is
entitled to withdraw the amount of Rs.10 Crores along with
accrued interest thereon, which is lying deposited with the
Registrar of learned City Civil Court, Calcutta, pursuant
to the order dated 17.03.2016 passed in C.O. No. 893 of
2016 passed by the Calcutta High Court.
5. We  are  not  reproducing  the  entire  terms  of
settlement,  however,  the  deed  of  settlement  executed
between the parties and annexed to IA No. 131814 of 2026,
2

--- Page 590 ---
shall be read as part of this order.
6. Learned counsel for the parties would jointly submit
that all the agreed terms of settlement has been acted upon
and Life Insurance Corporation of India shall be entitled
to withdraw the sum of Rs.10 Crores along with accrued
interest thereon as mentioned supra. 
7. Life Insurance Corporation of India may withdraw the
sum at the earliest, preferably within one month.
8. In view of the above, IA No. 131814 of 2026 is
allowed. The terms of settlement is recorded and made part
of this order.
9. The Special Leave Petition is disposed of in the
above terms.
10. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed
of. 
(MINI)                                 (AKSHAY KUMAR BHORIA)

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                           O R D E R
I.A. No.85179 of 2026
The prayer made in this application is for permitting
substituted service of unserved respondent Nos.5 to 7 through
the mode of paper publication. 
2. It  was  submitted  by  the  learned  advocate  for  the
applicant that despite best efforts, the above respondent
Nos.5 to 7 could not be served, therefore, publication through
newspapers  named  ‘Capital  Information’  (English)  and

--- Page 598 ---
‘Prajadarbar’ (Telugu) may be permitted.
3. Respondent Nos. 5 to 7 are permitted to be served by
publishing  notice  in  the  aforesaid  newspapers.  For  that
purpose, the Registry shall issue notice returnable fixing a
date after 10 weeks. In the notice to be so issued, it shall
be stated in addition to the mentioning of the date of posting
of the Transfer Petition, that on the next date, even if above
respondent Nos.5 to 7 do not remain present, the Court will
proceed  to  consider  the  matter  even  in  the  absence  of
aforesaid respondent Nos.5 to 7 to pass orders in accordance
with law.
4. The  applicant  shall  cooperate  with  the  Registry  in
completing  the  formalities  for  the  purposes  of  paper
publication of the notice. 
5. The application is accordingly allowed.
I.A. No.304362 of 2025
What is prayed in this application is to permit discharge
of learned advocate Ms. Rashmi Singhania, who appears as
advocate-on-record for the petitioner in Transfer Petition
(Crl.) Nos.340-41 of 2024.
When the application is considered, it has been found
that the instant AOR has given a No Objection Certificate
(NOC) to the petitioner as well as to the newly engaged
learned counsel for the petitioner.

--- Page 599 ---
In view of above and considering the averments made in
the application, the prayer for discharge of learned advocate
is granted.
Application is allowed.

India, New Delhi has filed an affidavit, relevant

IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CIVIL ORIGINAL JURISDICTION
MISCELLANEOUS APPLICATION NO. 2035 OF 2025
IN
SMW (CIVIL) NO. 4 OF 2024
IN RE REFIXATION OF PENSION CONSIDERING SERVICE PERIOD IN DISTRICT
JUDICIARY AND HIGH COURT
O R D E R
1. We have heard learned Attorney General for India as well as
the learned Amicus Curiae.
2. Paragraph Nos. 76 (i), (ii), and (x) of the judgment dated
19.05.2025 passed in SMW (Civil) No. 4 of 2024 are clarified to the
extent that the revised full pension mentioned in paragraph Nos. 76
(i) and (ii) shall be admissible w.e.f.  01.01.2016. The arrears
shall, thus, be computed accordingly.
3. In the event a Judge has retired before 01.01.2016, the
arrears up to 01.01.2016 shall be calculated on the basis of the
maximum pension admissible to a retired Chief Justice or Judge, as
the case may be, at the relevant time.
4. The  Miscellaneous  Application,  as  also  all  pending
interlocutory applications, stands disposed of accordingly.

in favour of the Ajit Singh. The same has been

ITEM NO.81               COURT NO.13               SECTION II
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Criminal Appeal  No(s).  4256/2025
VODAPELLY NARESH                                   Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF TELANGANA                                 Respondent(s)
[ ONLY IA NO. 168207/2026 IN Crl.A. No. 4257/2025 IS LISTED UNDER 
THIS ITEM ] 
 
WITH
Crl.A. No. 4257/2025 (II)
JOGU SURESH @ CHINNA  Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF TELANGANA                                 Respondent(s)
FOR GRANT OF BAIL ON IA 168207/2026
IA No. 168207/2026 - GRANT OF BAIL
 
Date : 27-05-2026 This appeal was called for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AHSANUDDIN AMANULLAH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE R. MAHADEVAN
For Appellant(s) : Ms. Manjeet Chawla, AoR
                   
                   Mr. Praveen Chaturvedi, AoR
                   Mr. Praveen Chaturvedi, Adv.
                   Ms. Jyoti Chaturvedi, Adv.
                   
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Kumar Vaibhaw, Adv.
                   Ms. Devina Sehgal, AoR
                   Mr. Dev Rishi Tyagi, Adv.
                   Mr. Yatharth Kansal, Adv.
                   
                  O R D E R
Criminal Appeal No. 4257/2025
IA No. 168207/2026
Heard learned counsel for the appellant. 

--- Page 625 ---
2. IA No. 168207/2026 has been filed seeking interim bail for the
appellant on the ground that his brother has died and since his
father is also dead, being the only male member, he has to perform
certain rituals.
3. At this juncture, we record our strong displeasure at the
pleadings made in the interlocutory application. At Paragraph No.4,
it is stated that there is no other male member in the house of the
appellant  to  perform  the  last  rites  of  the  brother  of  the
appellant.
4. We find this to be an absolutely incorrect statement. The last
rites have already been performed by some other person as the
brother was incarcerated. Thus, at best, it is the case that the
appellant has to participate in the rituals connected with the
death of his brother.
5. Be that as it may, purely on humanitarian grounds, the Court
has considered the matter.
6. Learned counsel for the State submitted that the fact of the
brother of the appellant having died has been verified and found to
be correct.
7. Having regard to the aforesaid, we grant interim bail to the
appellant for a period of two weeks, subject to conditions that may
be imposed by the Trial Court. The appellant shall surrender before
the concerned Superintendent of Police latest by 10.06.2026 and
proof of surrender be filed, latest by 15.07.2026.
8. IA No. 168207/2026 stands disposed of in the aforementioned

--- Page 626 ---
terms.
9. List on 29.07.2026.
(SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA) (ANJALI PANWAR)
   COURT MASTER (SH)              ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

ITEM NO.53               COURT NO.5               SECTION II
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s)  for  Special  Leave  to  Appeal  (Crl.)   No(s).
4882/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  21-05-
2018  in  CRLA  No.  1094/2016  passed  by  the  High  Court  of
Judicature at Allahabad]
PRAHLAD                                            Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF U.P                                       Respondent(s)
Date : 22-05-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE M.M. SUNDRESH
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE NONGMEIKAPAM KOTISWAR SINGH
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Ashutosh Yadav, Adv.
                   Mr. Yoshit Jain, Adv.
                   Mr. Gopal Singh, AOR
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Ruchira Goel, AOR
                   
                   
      UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Leave granted.
2. The  appellant  has  been  convicted  for  the  offences
punishable under Sections 302 and 323 read with Section 34, and
Sections 452, 504 and 506 of the Indian Penal Code, 1860 by the

--- Page 640 ---
2
Trial Court as confirmed by the High Court. The appellant has
been sentenced to undergo imprisonment and to pay fine along
with default stipulations, out of which the appellant has
undergone incarceration for a period of four years and six
months.
3. Heard the learned counsel appearing for the parties. 
4. It is not in dispute that the co-accused persons have been
granted the suspension of sentence during the pendency of their
appeals. 
5. As has rightly been submitted by the learned counsel
appearing  for  the  appellant,  we  do  find  mitigating
circumstances which are available on record. The occurrence was
in the year 2012, and there is no material to suggest that the
appellant was involved in any other similar offence. Now, more
than 10 years have elapsed since the date of the alleged
occurrence. The appellant has already been under incarceration
for  a  period  of  more  than  four  years  and  six  months.
Considering the aforesaid circumstances, we are inclined to
suspend the sentence imposed upon the appellant pending the
final disposal of the present appeal.
6. Accordingly, the sentence imposed by the Trial Court is

--- Page 641 ---
3
hereby suspended during the pendency of the present appeal. The
appellant shall be released on bail forthwith, subject to the
terms  and  conditions  imposed  to  the  satisfaction  of  the
concerned Trial Court.
7. The appeal shall be listed in due course.
(SACHIN KUMAR SRIVASTAVA)                       (POONAM VAID)
       COURT MASTER (SH)                        ASSISTANT  REGISTRAR

--- Page 642 ---
1
    IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA
CRIMINAL APPELLATE JURISDICTION
CRIMINAL APPEAL NO. 2785 OF 2026 
(Arising out of SLP (Crl.) No.19759 of 2025)
JAKIR ANSARI                            Appellant(s)
             
                   VERSUS
THE STATE OF JHARKHAND                             Respondent(s)
O R D E R
1. Heard learned counsel for the parties. 
2. Leave granted.
3. The appellant stood charged and convicted for the offences
punishable under Sections 379 and 354 of the Indian Penal Code,
1860. The conviction of the Trial Court has been confirmed
under the impugned judgment. Accordingly, the appellant has
been sentenced to rigorous imprisonment for a period of three
years out of which the appellant has undergone incarceration
for more than one year and ten months.
4. As rightly submitted by the learned counsel appearing for
the appellant, we do find mitigating circumstances available on

--- Page 643 ---
2
record. The occurrence was in the year 2006, and there is no
material to suggest that the appellant was involved in any
other similar offence. Now, more than 20 years have elapsed. He
has already been under incarceration for more than one year and
ten months. Considering the above, we confirm the conviction.
The sentence is modified to the period of incarceration already
undergone.
5. Accordingly, while confirming the conviction, the impugned
judgment stands set side to the extent of sentence imposed
alone. The appellant shall be released forthwith, unless he is
required in any other case.
6. Consequently, the appeal stands partly allowed.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of..
          
                                        .......................J.
(M.M. SUNDRESH)
                          .......................J.
(NONGMEIKAPAM KOTISWAR SINGH)
NEW DELHI
May 22, 2026.

BALU @ BALAMURUGAN                                 PETITIONER(S)

MADHUKAR G ANGUR & ANR.                            Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
REGISTRAR OF COMPANIES KARNATAKA & ORS.            Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
 
Date : 26-05-2026 These petitions were called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE DIPANKAR DATTA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Raghenth Basant, Sr. Adv.
                   Ms. Ashima Mandla, AOR
                   Ms. Mandakini Singh, Adv.
                   Mr. Ayush Shrivastava, Adv.
                   Ms. Hima Bhardwaj, Adv.
                   Ms. Sristhi Agarwal, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Debol Banerjee, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Shyam Sundar, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Sanjay Nuli, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Sharan Dev Singh Thakur, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Mahesh Thakur, AOR
                   Mr. Ayushi Srivastava, Adv.
                   Mr. Siddhartha Sati, Adv.
                   Mr. Narveer Yadav, Adv.
                   Ms. Ruchi Kumari, Adv.
                                      
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             
O R D E R
1. We have heard Mr. Raghenth Basant, learned senior counsel appearing for
the petitioners and Mr. Debol Banerjee, Mr. Shyam Sundar, and Sanjay Nuli,
learned senior counsel appearing for the respondents at some length. 

--- Page 780 ---
2
2. The common order dated 7 th April, 2026, under challenge in these special
leave petitions  passed by a learned Judge  of  the High  Court of Karnataka,
permits  several  writ  petitions  to  be  withdrawn  after  the  respective  writ
petitioners had obtained interim relief and had continued to reap the benefit
thereof from 2019 to 2026. Objection raised by the petitioners in these special
leave petitions did not yield fruit. 
3. Bearing in mind such fact as well as the facts that the petitioners before
us have invoked the writ jurisdiction of the High Court by instituting a fresh writ
petition1 and also that there are other proceedings pending before the relevant
National Company Law Tribunal/National Company Law Appellate Tribunal as
well as before the High Court by and between the parties, we clarify that such
interim relief (granted in 2019) shall not be called in aid in any pending or
future proceedings and will not enure to the benefit of the writ petitioners.
4. We grant liberty to the petitioners to amend Writ Petition No. 17360 of
2025. They are also permitted to raise all points of objection as raised in the
special leave petitions as well as establish the prejudice that they might have
suffered by reason of continuation of the order granting interim relief till it stood
vacated with the dismissal of the writ petitions.
5. All points and contentions on merits are left open.
6. The special leave petitions are disposed of on the above terms.
7. Pending interlocutory application(s), if any, shall also stand disposed of.
(JATINDER KAUR)                             (SUDHIR KUMAR SHARMA)
P.S. to REGISTRAR                             COURT MASTER (NSH)
1 Writ Petition No. 17360 of 2025

--- Page 781 ---

ITEM NO.57               COURT NO.12               SECTION II-D
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.) No. 9625/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 07-05-2026
in BA No. 1696/2026 passed by the High Court of Delhi at New Delhi]
SOBTI KOMAL                                        Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF NCT OF DELHI                              Respondent(s)
(IA No. 158322/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED
JUDGMENT)
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KUMAR
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) Mr. Arun Sharma, Adv.
                   Mr. Mirazuddin, Adv.
                   Mr. Bishnu Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Jitendra Kumar Deo, Adv.
                   Mr. Gurrick Jassar, Adv.
                   M/s V. Maheshwari & Co., AOR
For Respondent(s)  Mr. S.D. Sanjay, ASG
Mr. Kushal Kolwar, Adv. 
Mr. Akshat Agrawal, Adv. 
Mr. M.P. Gupta, Adv. 
Mr. Shubh Sharma, Adv. 
       UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
We find no reason to interfere with the impugned order. The
High Court specifically recorded the statement made by the learned
Assistant  Public  Prosecutor,  on  instructions,  from  the
Investigating Officer (I.O), that the offer made by the petitioner,
Sobit  Komal,  that  she  would  join  and  participate  in  the
investigation was acceptable. 
1

--- Page 792 ---
Based on the same, the High Court had directed the petitioner,
Sobit Komal, to join and participate in the investigation on
08.05.2026. However, the petitioner, Sobit Komal, failed to do so
under the impression that no protection was afforded to her from
being arrested. This apprehension on her part was completely
unfounded in the light of what we have noted hereinbefore. 
In the light of what has been stated above, the petitioner,
Komal Sobit, shall join and cooperate with the investigation, by
presenting herself before the I.O. on 01.06.2026 at 4.00 p.m.
Needless to state, she would have to appear as and when called
upon to do so by the I.O. 
With  the  above  observations,  the  special  leave  petition
disposed of. 
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (BABITA PANDEY)                                (PREETI SAXENA)
     AR-CUM-PS                           COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

--- Page 793 ---

the High Court; hence, the special leave petition is dismissed.
3. However,  subject  to  the  petitioner  acquiring  eligibility  for  pre-mature

release.  An appropriate decision shall be taken by the Department without any
delay, once the Superintendent moves on behalf of the petitioner for securing

--- Page 851 ---
IN THE SUPREME COURT OF INDIA 
CIVIL APPELLATE JURISDICTION 
CIVIL APPEAL No.             OF  2026
                       [@ Civil Appeal Diary No. 24841 OF 2026]                          
 
UNION OF INDIA                                     APPELLANT
                                VERSUS
KANNIAH ANANDH & ORS.                                  RESPONDENTS
O R D E R
1. Delay condoned.
2.    Since the order under challenge was passed by the National Company
Law Appellate Tribunal1, Chennai without hearing the appellant - Union of India2,
we grant liberty to the UOI to file an application for recall.
3. We note, a Full Bench of the NCLAT in its decision reported in  Union
Bank of India vs. Dinkar T. Venkatasubramanian & Ors. 3 has held that
although the NCLAT has no authority to review its order, it has the competence
to hear an application for recall.
4. The appeal is disposed of on the above terms.
5. Pending interlocutory application(s), if any, also stand disposed of.
…………….............................J.
[DIPANKAR DATTA]
……………..............................J.
[SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA]
New Delhi;
May 22, 2026.

2  UOI
3  2023 SCC OnLine NCLAT 283

For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1) Delay condoned. 
2) Having heard learned counsel for the petitioner, we do
not find any ground to interfere with the impugned order of
conviction.  However, it is stated that the petitioner has
already undergone more than 19 years incarceration and would
be entitled to grant of remission.  
3) Considering  the  aforesaid,  while  dismissing  this
special  leave  petition,  we  direct  that  in  case  the
petitioner  applies  for  grant  of  remission  before  the
competent  authority,  the  same  shall  be  considered  and
decided in accordance with law as expeditiously as possible,
1

--- Page 854 ---

preferably  within  four  months  of  the  filing  of  the
application.  
4) The special leave petition is, accordingly, dismissed.
5) Pending application(s), if any, stands disposed of.

                             O R D E R
The  additional  affidavit  filed  by  respondent  No.  1  on
26.05.2026 discloses a very disturbing turn of events.  An order
was passed by this Court on 19.01.2026 and the same has been
altered by adding a sentence therein. Copies of the original and
the manipulated order are placed on record. The distorted order is
at page 19 of the additional affidavit.
1

--- Page 874 ---
SLP(C) Nos. 33195-33196/2025
The Registry of this Court shall initiate an enquiry into the
matter  and  also  file  a  complaint  with  the  concerned  police
authorities in relation to the tampering with this Court’s order,
so that responsibility can be affixed on those who are behind the
same.  In the event, any employee in the Registry of this Court is
also involved in this misadventure, necessary disciplinary action
would have to be taken against him/her.
The enquiry in this regard shall be undertaken expeditiously
and a report shall be placed before this Court.
Re-list on 15.09.2026.

Court for appropriate directions in relation to visitation rights
and also for personal interaction with the children in the Court
premises in the presence of a female staff member on 30.05.2026.

ITEM NO.805               COURT NO.15               SECTION II-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Criminal Appeal  No(s).  2667/2026 
(Arising out of Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.) 
No(s). 5068/2026)
PAPPU ALIAS SUHAS KISAN SONWALKAR                   Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF MAHARASHTRA                           Respondent(s)
 
Date : 27-05-2026 This appeal was mentioned today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARAVIND KUMAR
         HON'BLE  MR. JUSTICE PRASANNA B. VARALE
For Appellant(s) : Mr. Anand Dilip Landge, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : Mr. Aaditya Aniruddha Pande, AOR
                   
                   
          UPON being mentioned, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1.  Upon being mentioned, the matter is taken on board. 
2. Learned counsel for the appellant submits that vide order
dated 18.05.2026, appellant was granted bail by this Court but as
regards the name of the appellant there is mismatch in the record
of proceedings. Instead of "Pappu alias Suhas Kisan Sonwalkar" it
records the name as "Pappu alais Suhan Kisan Sonwakar". He prays
for correction of name in the record of proceedings. 
3. On going through the petition filed by the appellant, we
observe that the name of the appellant itself is written wrong in
1 of 2

--- Page 934 ---
Criminal Appeal  No(s).  2667/2026
the memo of parties filed before this Court and would add that in
the impugned order name is correctly spelt. Hence, he seeks for
correction. However, keeping in mind that the appellant has been
granted bail, we direct the Trial Court and concerned authorities
that the name of the appellant shall be read as "Pappu alias Suhas
Kisan Sonwalkar" instead of "Pappu alais Suhan Kisan Sonwakar".
(KAPIL TANDON)                                  (AVGV RAMU)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Having regard to the peculiar facts of the instant case
namely the intra-court appeal having been dismissed on the ground
of delay and the explanation offered thereunder as well as the
explanation offered before this Court for condoning the delay of
351 days, would not constitute “sufficient cause” as prescribed
under Section 5 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

ground of delay and we make it clear that we have not expressed any

WITH
SLP(Crl) No. 18547/2025 (II-E)
FOR ADMISSION and I.R.
IA No. 295492/2025 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT and IA No. 295491/2025 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 25-05-2026 These matters were called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ARAVIND KUMAR
         HON'BLE  MR. JUSTICE PRASANNA B. VARALE
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Varun Thakur, Adv.
                   Mr. Ramkaran, Adv.
                   Mr. Deepak Goel, Adv.
                   Mr. M.K.Ravi, Adv.
                   Ms. Tanuj Bagga Sharma, Adv.
                   Ms. Vishakha Singh Chauhan, Adv.
                   Mr. S P Singh, Adv.
                   Mrs. Shraddha Saran, Adv.
                   M/s Varun Thakur & Associates, AOR
                   
                   
For Respondent(s) :Ms. Mrinal Gopal Elker, AOR
                   Mr. V.V.V. Pattabhiram, Adv.
                   Ms. Chhavi Khandelwal, Adv.
                   Mr. Manish Yadav, Adv.
                   
                   

--- Page 992 ---
         
         UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The present petition has been filed challenging one of the
conditions imposed for grant of bail on the ground of same being
onerous whereunder the High Court has directed the appellant-
accused to deposit Rs.36,68,250/- (Rupees Thirty Six Lacs Sixty
Eight Thousand and Two Hundred Fifty only). This condition was
imposed on the ground that learned counsel appearing for the
petitioner himself had undertaken to deposit the whole amount.
2. Learned counsel appearing for the petitioner would vehemently
contend that the said amount has already been paid subsequent to
the order of the High Court to all the complainants. The affidavit
and the document annexed are as vague as vagueness could be.
3. In that view of the matter, we direct the petitioner to specify
in a particular statement by indexing it with the name of the
complainant, the amount which according to the complainant is to be
due and how it has been paid i.e. on what date and through which
bank, by way of a tabular column. On filing of such statement, the
prayer of the petitioner, would be examined. 
4. List after partial court working days/summer vacation, 2026. 
(RASHI GUPTA)                                   (AVGV RAMU)
COURT MASTER (SH)                             COURT MASTER (NSH)

Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Pranav Sarthi, AOR
                   Mr. Utkarsh Vatsa, Adv.
                   Mr. Ayush Raj, Adv.
                   Ms. Prachi Dhingra, Adv.
                   Ms. Udit Bajpai, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Davinder Pal Singh, ASG
Mr. Digvijay Singh, Adv.
Ms. Vidhi Gupta, Adv.

Mr. Vivek Gurnani, Adv.
Mr.Digvijay Dam, Adv.
Mr. B.K. Satija, Adv.
 Mr. Shantanu Kumar, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The learned counsel for the petitioner submits that while
rejecting the bail prayer of respondent, the Trial Court had taken
note of Investigating Officer collecting recordings of certain
calls, wherein the deceased has complained of harassment. The
1

--- Page 1028 ---
transcripts of those calls are not on record.
2. The learned counsel for the petitioner, accordingly, prays for

RAJULAL                                            Petitioner(s)

STATE OF RAJASTHAN                                 Respondent(s)
(FOR ADMISSION

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE S.V.N. BHATTI
For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Namit Saxena, AOR

                   Ms. Sonali Gaur, Adv.
                   Mr. Saurabh Rajpal, Adv.
                   Ms. Nidhi Jaswal, AOR                  
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Heard learned counsel for the parties.
2. The petitioner is convicted under Sections
363, 366, 343, 376(3) of the Indian Penal Code,
1860 and Sections 3/4(ii) of the  Protection of
Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012  and has
been  sentenced  to  twenty  years  of  rigorous
imprisonment.

--- Page 1104 ---
2
3. The  application  of  the  petitioner  for
suspension of sentence pending his criminal appeal
before the High Court has been rejected by the
impugned order.
4. The  petitioner  has  hardly  suffered
incarceration  for  about  three  years  and  three
months.
5. In the facts and circumstances of the case
and the findings which have been returned by the
Trial Court, we are not inclined to interfere with
the order impugned at this stage.
6. The special leave petition is, accordingly,
dismissed.
7. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand
disposed of.

For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The  instant  writ  petition,  under  Article  32  of  the
Constitution of India, has been filed seeking a direction for the
early disposal of the writ petition filed by the petitioner, which
is pending before the Allahabad High Court. While we are not
inclined to entertain such a writ petition, we grant liberty to the
petitioner to move an application before the High Court and make
out a case for an out-of-turn hearing of his writ petition. 
2. We have no reason to doubt that such a prayer will be
considered by the High Court sympathetically.
3. The Writ Petition is, accordingly, dismissed with liberty as

aforesaid.
4. All pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.

               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Writ Petition(s)(Civil) No(s).658/2026
SHOBHA GUPTA & ORS.                                Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
UNION OF INDIA & ORS.                              Respondent(s)
Date : 27-05-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIPUL M. PANCHOLI
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Shobha Gupta, Adv.
                   Ms. Akshita Mishra, Adv.
                   Ms. Simranjeet Kaur, Adv.
                   Ms. Manasvi Negi, Adv.
                   Mr. Birender Sangwan, Adv.
                   Mr. Gaurav Meena, Adv.
                   Mr. Ronak Karanpuria, AOR
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. We appreciate the initiative taken by the petitioners through
the instant writ petition, where they want to impress upon the
members of the legal fraternity to join the court proceedings
through virtual hearings/online. However, it may not be prudent or
expedient for this Court to issue a judicial command compelling all
members to appear online, without knowing the difficulties or
impediments that individual members, especially the young members
of the Bar, might be facing. 
2. Consequently, we do not deem it appropriate to issue any
direction.  However,  the  earnest  appeal  issued  through  two
Administrative Circulars needs to be reminded. This Court will
1

--- Page 1140 ---
welcome each and every member of the Bar/legal fraternity who
decides to attend the Court proceedings through virtual/online
mode.
3. With these observations/clarifications, the Writ Petition is
disposed of. Pending applications, if any, also stand closed.
(ARJUN BISHT)                                (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

ITEM NO.40               COURT NO.1               SECTION IV-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C) No(s).18046/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 04-03-2026
in WP No.3570/2025 passed by the High Court of Karnataka at
Bengaluru]
SURI PAYALA                                        Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF KARNATAKA                                 Respondent(s)
IA No. 156413/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED
JUDGMENT, IA No. 156416/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. K. Parameshwar, Sr. Adv.
                   Mr. Sarthak Srivastava, Adv.
                   Ms. Sunetra Goswami, Adv.
                   Mr. Rishab Trakroo, Adv.
                   Ms. Sri Vallari K N, Adv.
                   Mr. Vatsal Joshi, AOR                   
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The petitioner, who is serving as the Engineer-in-Chief in the
Karnataka State Pollution Control Board, filed a purported PIL
challenging  the  appointment  of  various  MLAs/MLCs  as
Chairperson/President/Board Members in the State-owned Corporations
and Boards on the ground that all such MLAs/MLCs have been granted
benefits of Cabinet Ministers in gross violation of Article 164(1A)
of the Constitution of India.
2. The High Court dismissed the petition primarily on the ground
1

--- Page 1172 ---
of questioning the petitioner’s bona fides, while observing that
the petitioner himself was an aspirant for certain posts. It has
been noticed that, earlier, the petitioner was appointed as Member
Secretary of the State Pollution Control Board, but was removed
from that post, apparently on account of certain irregularities. 
3. It seems to us that, irrespective of the petitioner’s own
locus or conduct, the Government Order dated 07.10.2020, comprising
proceedings of the State Government (Annexure P/1 of the instant
paperbook), deserves a deeper analysis. The petitioner and his
counsel apparently did not lay much emphasis on the contents of the
said Government Order, which might have some bearing on the issue
sought to be raised. 
4. Consequently, we dispose of this Special Leave Petition with
liberty to the petitioner to file a review petition before the High
Court,  inter alia,  pointing out how the Government Order dated
07.10.2020 is an attempt to overreach the constitutional mandate
contained in Article 164(1A) of the Constitution.
5. It is clarified that we have not expressed any opinion on the
merits of the case.
6. Similarly, this order may not be construed as having accorded
locus standi to the petitioner to file a PIL.
7. Liberty, however, is granted to approach this Court again if
the review petition is dismissed.
8. All pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.
(ARJUN BISHT)                                (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        ASSISTANT REGISTRAR
2

--- Page 1173 ---

          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. In purported compliance with the order dated 10.02.2026, the
Gujarat Pollution Control Board has filed an affidavit/compliance-
cum-status report. It is claimed that the said Board has prepared a
comprehensive and robust action plan for the scientific utilization
of funds to the tune of Rs.13.3 crores for the purposes of
environmental improvement and restoration. It is further averred
that a detailed expenditure report will be submitted to the
appropriate forum upon completion of the works and utilization of
the funds. 
2. While we do not wish to make any adverse observations against
the Pollution Control Board for non-disclosure of the plan said to
have been prepared for utilization of the funds, nevertheless we
deem it appropriate to direct the Member Secretary of the Board to
place the same on record along with an affidavit within four weeks.
No further opportunity shall be granted.
