(SLP/TP/WP/..)
 
Date : 27-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE J.K. MAHESHWARI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Sangeeta Singh, AOR
                   Ms. Twinkle Sharma, Adv.                   
                   
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Shantanu Sagar, AOR
                   Mr. Anil Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Abhishek Kumar Gupta, Adv.                  
                   
                   Mr. Subhro Sanyal, AOR
                   Ms. Anki Kashyap, Adv.
Ms. Nitu Sharma, Adv.
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. After hearing learned counsel for the complainant-petitioner
and the learned counsel for the State, we find that the eye
witnesses have deposed regarding the presence and assault made by

--- Page 1212 ---
2
the accused persons.  The injuries received to the injured is
grievous in nature.  Considering the same, in our view the
anticipatry bail as allowed by the High Court without taking note
of these facts is not justified.
2. It is further required to be noted that after issuance of the
notice by this Court, the service was made effective.  Therefore on
24.04.2026, this Court was required to pass an order asking a
report.  Again, the accused-respondents 2 to 6 have not appeared.
On 06.05.2026,  bailable warrants were issued.  Only thereafter, on
07.05.2026, Vakalatnama was filed on behalf of Respondent Nos. 2,
3, 4 and 6.  Respondent No. 5 has not appeared either personally or
through advocate. We take the said conduct of the respondents on
record.
3. Considering all the attending circumstances, we are inclined
to cancel the anticipatory bail as granted by the High Court.  All
the accused persons, Respondent Nos. 2 to 6, are directed to
surrender within one week, otherwise the authorities are at liberty
to take recourse as permissible.
4. With the above direction, the special leave petition is
disposed of.  Pending applications, if any, shall stand disposed
of.
(GULSHAN KUMAR ARORA)                           (NAND KISHOR)
  DEPUTY REGISTRAR                           ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

Criminal Appeal  No(s).  2284/2026
SHAHRUKHKAN MOULANAIK PATIL                        Appellant(s)
                                VERSUS
THE STATE OF KARNATAKA                             Respondent(s)
Date : 27-05-2026 This appeal was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PAMIDIGHANTAM SRI NARASIMHA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ALOK ARADHE
For Appellant(s) : Mr. H. Chandra Sekhar, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s) : Mr. Nishanth Patil, A.A.G.
                   Mr. Sanchit Garga, AOR
                   Mr. Awanish Gupta, Adv.
                   Mr. Arijit Dey, Adv.
                   Ms. Bhumi Agrawal, Adv.
                   Mr. Abhishek Gupta, Adv.
                   Mr. Kunal Rana, Adv.
                   Mr. Shashwat Jaiswal, Adv.
                   
   UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This appeal is filed against the impugned order dated
04.07.2023 passed by the High Court of Karnataka, Bench at
Dharwad, in Criminal Appeal No. 100164 of 2018.
2. The Trial Court had acquitted the appellant of all the
charges in Sessions Case No. 42 of 2015.  However, the High
Court has allowed the State’s appeal and convicted the
appellant under Section 376 of the Indian Penal Code and
sentenced him to undergo rigorous imprisonment for a period
of 10 years.
3. Heard learned counsel appearing for the appellant and the

--- Page 1244 ---
2
learned Additional Advocate General appearing for the State.
4. The State has filed a short counter affidavit, paragraph
16 of which is extracted hereunder :-
“That  it  is  further  submitted  that  the
Petitioner has undergone a period of 2 years
11 months and 04 days including his under
trial period of 09 months and 20 days (from
02.12.2014  to  22.09.2015)  and  during  his
imprisonment  his  conduct  has  been  found
satisfactory and good.”
5.Having considered the matter in detail, we are of the
opinion that since the appellant has already undergone
around 03 years of incarceration, he should be released on
bail.  This will, however, be subject to such terms and
conditions as may be imposed by the trial court. Ordered
accordingly.
6.Hearing of the appeal is expedited.  
7.List  the  criminal  appeal  for  final  disposal  in  the
Regular Hearing list in the month of September, 2026.
 (JAYANT KUMAR ARORA)                           (NIDHI WASON)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                       ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

ITEM NO.2                   COURT NO.8                 SECTION II-A
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.) No.7117/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 17-02-2025 
in CRM No. 80518/2024 passed by the High Court of Judicature at 
Patna]
NITESH KUMAR                                          Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF BIHAR                                        Respondent(s)
IA No. 107252/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 25-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE DIPANKAR DATTA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. Priyanshu Tueriyar, Adv.
                   Mr. Yogesh Vats, Adv.
                   Mr. Siddhartha Sinha, AOR
                                      
For Respondent(s) :Mr. Azmat Hayat Amanullah, AOR
                   Ms. Rebecca Mishra, Adv.
                                      
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Having perused the statement of the victim recorded under
Section 164 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973, we are not
inclined to interfere with the impugned judgment and order of the
High Court; hence, the special leave petition is dismissed.
2. Mr.  Azmat Hayat Amanullah, learned counsel appearing for
the State, submits that charges had not been framed because of
pendency of a discharge application under Section 227 of the Code of
Criminal  Procedure,  1973  filed  by  the  petitioner;  however,  the
discharge application has been dismissed recently and the court will
1

--- Page 1390 ---
be framing charges shortly.
3. We  encourage  the  trial  court  to  proceed  with  framing  of
charges expeditiously as well as conduct and conclude the trial as per
law upon recording the evidence of the five witnesses the prosecution
proposes to examine. Evidence of defence witness, if any, may also
be recorded. 
4. If there be any change of circumstances, or no reasonable
progress to conclude the trial is achieved within six months next from
date  of commencement of  witness  action  despite  full  cooperation
extended by the petitioner for concluding the trial, he shall be at
liberty to renew his prayer for bail before the relevant court.
5. All contentions on merits are left open.
6. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(KRITIKA TIWARI)                              (SUDHIR KUMAR SHARMA)
SENIOR PERSONAL ASSISTANT                       COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. Delay condoned.
2. The submission of the learned counsel for
the petitioner is that the trial court decree dated
12.12.2009 is conditional in the sense that it
provided that the plaintiff shall pay the balance
sale consideration to the defendant, or deposit the
1

--- Page 1422 ---
same in court, within two months from the date of
the decree, failing which the suit shall stand
automatically dismissed. Admittedly the plaintiff
did not make deposit of the requisite amount within
the specified period, and no application was made
to the Court that passed the decree to extend the
time for deposit. In such circumstances, the decree
became null and inexecutable.
2. Issue notice, returnable in eight weeks.
3. In the meantime, the effect and operation of
the High Court’s order shall remain stayed.

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) : 
                   Dr. Vinod Kumar Tewari, AOR
                   Mr. Syed Mohd. Muztaba, Adv.

For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. By the impugned order High Court quashed the
prosecution under Sections 3(1)(r) and 3(1)(s) of
the  Scheduled  Castes  and  Scheduled  Tribes
1

--- Page 1426 ---
(Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989 (“SC & ST Act”)
but permitted prosecution for offences punishable
under Sections 420 and 504 of the Indian Penal Code,
1860 (“IPC”) and Section 3(2)(va) of the SC & ST Act
to continue. 
2. It is the case of the petitioner that Section
3(2)(va) of the SC & ST Act would be applicable only
if  the  offence  committed  is  specified  in  the
Schedule  to  the  Act.  It  is  the  case  of  the
petitioner  that  the  offences  punishable  under
Sections 420 and 504 IPC are not specified in the
Schedule to the Act and, therefore, Section 3(2)(va)
is not attracted. 
3. Issue  notice,  limited  to  the  extent  of
applicability of Section 3(2)(va) of the SC & ST
Act, returnable on 14.07.2026.      
(CHETAN ARORA)                                  (SAPNA BANSAL)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

                             O R D E R
1. Delay condoned.
2. Heard learned counsel for the parties.
3. The petitioner has been convicted under the NDPS Act and had
been sentenced to 10 years of rigorous imprisonment with fine of
Rs. 1,00,000/- (Rs. One Lakh only). In default in payment of fine,
he is directed to undergo a further imprisonment of one year. The
aforesaid conviction and sentence has been upheld by the High
Court.

--- Page 1446 ---
2
4. The petitioner has already undergone more than 9 years 9
months of the aforesaid imprisonment.
5. The submission of learned counsel for the petitioner is that
he is a very poor person and he is not in a position to pay the
fine and that for this reason he has to unnecessarily undergo one
additional year of imprisonment.
6. Learned counsel for the State leaves it upon the Court so as
to reduce the fine keeping in mind the capacity of the petitioner
to pay. Thus, in the facts and circumstances of the case and
considering the situation of the petitioner, we reduce the fine to
Rs. 25,000/- (Rs. Twenty Five Thousand only) to be paid by the
petitioner before the completion of the sentence of 10 years. In
the event, the fine is not paid by the petitioner within the above
time, he would undergo the default sentence of one year in
addition.
7. The Special Leave Petition stands disposed of.
8. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(KANCHAN CHOUHAN)                               (NIDHI MATHUR)
SENIOR PERSONAL ASSISTANT                     COURT MASTER (NSH)

--- Page 1447 ---
1

1. On one of the previous occasions, i.e. on 19.01.2026,
1

--- Page 1460 ---
SLP (CRL.)  NO(S).  14138/2025
we had granted time to the prosecution to complete the
examination of 15 material witnesses within a period of
four months. However, till date no material witness has
been examined.
2. Learned counsel for the State of Gujarat informs that
in the State Special Courts are being created for trial
of the NDPS cases and on account of this process, though
a Court has been created but huge number of cases have
been assigned to one particular Court, for which reason
it was not possible to take up the matter.
3. In view of the above, we want the State to come out
with the figures as to how many Special Courts have been
created and how many cases have been assigned to each of
the Court and whether for each of the Court so created,
sufficient  number  of  Public  Prosecutors  have  been
assigned.
4. Learned counsel for the State of Gujarat may seek
instructions on the above aspects.
5. List on 13.07.2026.

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE NONGMEIKAPAM KOTISWAR SINGH

                   Ms. Mini Gangadharan, Adv.
                   Mr. Devesh Maurya, Adv.

                   Mr. Ravi Kumar, Adv.
                   Ms. Ashu Tomar, Adv.
                   Mr. Vikash Kumar, Adv.
                   Mr. Sunny Kapoor, Adv.
                   
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
We have heard the learned counsel appearing for the petitioner

--- Page 1572 ---
and learned counsel appearing for the respondents.
Learned counsel appearing for respondents Nos. 1 and 2 submits
that the statement made as recorded in the order dated 25.02.2026
does not apply to respondents Nos. 1 and 2, though the learned
counsel appearing for respondents Nos. 1 and 2 submits that he is
also representing respondent No.3. 
Another counsel representing respondent No.3 reiterates the
earlier statement made, as recorded in the order dated 25.02.2026. 
Suffice it is to say that in so far as respondent No.3 is
concerned, the stand is consistent. 
However, in view of the clear stand taken by the respondents
who are the plaintiffs that they have not given any such statement,
we are inclined to recall the order dated 25.02.2026 passed by this
Court.
Accordingly, the Special Leave Petition is restored on the
file.
All interim orders are also automatically restored. 
List the Special Leave Petition on 03.08.2026.

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AUGUSTINE GEORGE MASIH

     UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following

PRAVEEN KUMAR SINGH                                Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
JANARDAN NIRMAN PVT. LTD. & ORS.                   Respondent(s)
FOR ADMISSION 
IA No. 65770/2026 - CONDONATION OF DELAY IN REFILING/CURING THE 
DEFECTS
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KUMAR
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s)  Mr. Mayank Kshirsagar, AOR
                   
For Respondent(s)  Mr. Rohan Thawani, Adv.
                   Mr. V. Shyamohan, Adv.
                   Ms. Priyanka Agarwal, Adv.
                   Ms. Anshika Bajpai, Adv.
                   Ms. Vrinda Goel, Adv.
                   M/S. Kmnp Law Aor, AOR
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R

                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
PETITION(S) FOR SPECIAL LEAVE TO APPEAL (C)  NO(S).  17360/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  30-01-2026
in RVW No. 2/2022 passed by the High Court of Calcutta Circuit
Bench at Port Blair]
S. SIMACHALAM                                      Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
UNION OF INDIA & ORS.                              Respondent(s)
IA No. 148593/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANOJ MISRA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE MANMOHAN 
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. P.C.Das, Adv.
                   Mr. Sukesh Ghosh, Adv.
                   Mr. Ashis Kumar Chowdhary, Adv.
                   Ms. Sadhana Sandhu, AOR
                                      
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This special leave petition impugns two orders.  First is
dated 19.08.2019 and the second is dated 30.01.2026.  Against the
first order, SLP (Civil) No.20583/2021 was dismissed by this Court
without  giving  liberty  to  challenge  the  said  order  afresh.
Thereafter, the review was dismissed by the second impugned order.
2. Having regard to the decision of this Court in Satheesh V.K.
vs. Federal Bank Ltd. (2025 SCC OnLine SC 2046), this special leave
petition is not maintainable.  It is dismissed as such.
3. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (KAVITA PAHUJA)                               (SAPNA BANSAL)

ITEM NO.5               COURT NO.1               SECTION X
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Writ Petition(s)(Civil) No(s).667/2026
KUNWER HIRESH SARAN SAXENA                         Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF UTTAR PRADESH & ORS.                      Respondent(s)
IA No. 160449/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING O.T.
 
Date : 27-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE THE CHIEF JUSTICE
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE JOYMALYA BAGCHI
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE VIPUL M. PANCHOLI
For Petitioner(s) :Ms. Vanshaja Shukla, Adv.
                   Ms. C. Ankeeta Appanna, AOR
                   Ms. Madhura Chitnis, Adv.
                   Mr. Siddhant Yadav, Adv.
                   Mr. Ishit Mahajan, Adv.
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. The  instant  writ  petition,  under  Article  32  of  the
Constitution of India, has been filed seeking a direction for the
early disposal of the writ petition filed by the petitioner, which
is pending before the Allahabad High Court. While we are not
inclined to entertain such a writ petition, we grant liberty to the
petitioner to move an application before the High Court along with
the requisite medical record to make out a case for an out-of-turn
hearing of his writ petition. 
2. We have no reason to doubt that such a prayer will be
considered by the High Court sympathetically.
3. The Writ Petition is, accordingly, dismissed with liberty as
aforesaid.
4. All pending applications, if any, also stand disposed of.
(ARJUN BISHT)                                (PREETHI DILEEP KUMAR)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        ASSISTANT REGISTRAR

ITEM NO.5                  COURT NO.12                 SECTION II-B
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition for Special Leave to Appeal (Crl.) No. 5687/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 20-02-2026
in CRMM No. 61385/2025 passed by the High Court of Punjab & Haryana
at Chandigarh]
KARAMVEER                                          Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
STATE OF HARYANA                                   Respondent(s)
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SANJAY KUMAR
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE K. VINOD CHANDRAN
For Petitioner(s) : Mr. Roopansh Purohit, AOR
                   Ms. Narayani Sepaha, Adv.                      
For Respondent(s) :  
                   Mr. Shekhar Raj Sharma, A.A.G.
                   Mr. Akshay Amritanshu, AOR
                   Ms. Nidhi Narwal, Adv.
                   Ms. Srishti Jain, Adv.
                   Mr. Sarthak Srivastava, Adv.                   
                   
          UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
As the petitioner, Karamveer, failed to disclose the fact that
an earlier First Information Report (FIR) was registered against
him in the year 2023, such non-disclosure would be fatal in view of
the law laid down by this Court in  Munnesh vs. State of Uttar
Pradesh1.
The special leave petition is, accordingly, dismissed on that
short ground.
Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(DEEPAK GUGLANI)                             (PREETI SAXENA)
DEPUTY REGISTRAR                         COURT MASTER (NSH)
1   2025 SCC OnLine SC 1319

ITEM NO.32               COURT NO.6               SECTION XIV
               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
Petition(s) for Special Leave to Appeal (C)  No(s).  17344/2026
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated 08-05-2026  
in CM(M) No. 1063/2026 passed by the High Court of Delhi at New  
Delhi]
GARUDA CONSTRUCTION AND ENGINEERS PVT. LTD         Petitioner(s)
                                VERSUS
DINESH KHANNA                                      Respondent(s)
(IA No. 148412/2026 - EXEMPTION FROM FILING C/C OF THE IMPUGNED 
JUDGMENT)
 
Date : 26-05-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE PAMIDIGHANTAM SRI NARASIMHA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ALOK ARADHE
For Petitioner(s): Mr. Soayib Qureshi, AOR
                   Mr. Varnit Vashistha, Adv.
                   Mr. Aman Qayoom Wani, Adv. 
  
For Respondent(s): 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. While we are not inclined to interfere with the  
order passed by the High Court, all questions of law  
and fact would be available to the petitioner, if he  
has  to  file  a  First  Appeal  against  the  impugned

1. Having regard to the submissions made by the learned counsel
for the petitioner and the facts of the case, we do not deem it
necessary to exercise our discretion under Article 136 of the
Constitution of India.
2. The Special Leave Petition is, accordingly, dismissed.
3. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
(KANCHAN CHOUHAN)                               (NIDHI MATHUR)
SENIOR PERSONAL ASSISTANT                     COURT MASTER (NSH)

                             O R D E R
